---
title: "Stage 2: SKA2 Alignment Exploration"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    theme: cosmo
    fig-responsive: true
jupyter: basicpython
params:
  build_dir: "../results/01_build"
  explore_dir: "../results/02_explore"
  run_name: "pipeline run"
  highlights_file: ""
---

<!--
Render command (use absolute paths — Quarto resolves paths relative to the notebook file):

  conda run -n quarto quarto render \
      /path/to/subspecies-phylogeny/notebooks/stage2_explore.qmd \
      -P build_dir:/absolute/path/to/run/results/01_build \
      -P explore_dir:/absolute/path/to/run/results/02_explore \
      -P run_name:Buniformis \
      --output-dir /absolute/path/to/run/notebooks/

  Example for Buniformis:

  conda run -n quarto quarto render \
      /Users/timrozday/Documents/subspecies-phylogeny/notebooks/stage2_explore.qmd \
      -P build_dir:/Users/timrozday/Documents/subspecies-phylogeny_runs/Buniformis/results/01_build \
      -P explore_dir:/Users/timrozday/Documents/subspecies-phylogeny_runs/Buniformis/results/02_explore \
      -P run_name:Buniformis \
      --output-dir /Users/timrozday/Documents/subspecies-phylogeny_runs/Buniformis/notebooks/

Tree visualisation: uses toytree + toyplot (installed in basicpython).
  Trees are saved as SVG and interactive HTML via toyplot renderers.

Static image export for Plotly figures (PNG/SVG) requires kaleido:
    mamba install -c conda-forge python-kaleido
  Without kaleido, interactive HTML files are saved instead.
-->

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| include: false
# Default parameter values — overridden by -P flags at render time
build_dir       = "../results/01_build"
explore_dir     = "../results/02_explore"
run_name        = "pipeline run"
highlights_file = ""
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Imports and parameter setup
# ============================================================
import sys
import warnings
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import yaml
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
import toytree
import toyplot
import toyplot.html
import toyplot.svg
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import plotly.io as pio
import re
import io

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Convert injected param strings to Path objects
build_dir   = Path(build_dir)
explore_dir = Path(explore_dir)
run_name    = str(run_name)

# ---- Highlights config -----------------------------------------------
_DEFAULT_PALETTE = ['#e63946', '#f4a261', '#2a9d8f', '#8338ec', '#fb8500']
highlights = {}   # name -> {'label': str, 'color': str}

_hf = str(highlights_file).strip()
if _hf:
    _path = Path(_hf)
    if _path.exists():
        _cfg = yaml.safe_load(_path.read_text()) or {}
        for i, entry in enumerate(_cfg.get('highlights', [])):
            name  = str(entry['name'])
            label = str(entry.get('label', name))
            color = str(entry.get('color', _DEFAULT_PALETTE[i % len(_DEFAULT_PALETTE)]))
            highlights[name] = {'label': label, 'color': color}


DEFAULT_TIP_COLOR = '#457b9d'


def tip_color(name):
    return highlights[name]['color'] if name in highlights else DEFAULT_TIP_COLOR

# Output directories co-located with the pipeline results
figs_dir   = explore_dir / "figs"   / "stage2"
tables_dir = explore_dir / "tables" / "stage2"
figs_dir.mkdir(parents=True,   exist_ok=True)
tables_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ---- Kaleido availability (for Plotly static export) ----------------
try:
    import kaleido  # noqa: F401
    HAS_KALEIDO = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_KALEIDO = False


def save_fig(fig_plotly, stem):
    """Save an interactive Plotly figure.

    Saves as interactive HTML always; also saves SVG/PNG if kaleido is
    installed (mamba install -c conda-forge python-kaleido).
    """
    if fig_plotly is None:
        return
    fig_plotly.write_html(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.html"))
    if HAS_KALEIDO:
        fig_plotly.write_image(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.svg"))
        fig_plotly.write_image(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.png"), scale=2)


PLOTLY_LAYOUT = dict(
    template='plotly_white',
    font=dict(family='Arial, sans-serif', size=13),
)
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Load all data upfront
# ============================================================

# --- FastANI -------------------------------------------------------
fastani_path = build_dir / "fastani" / "fastani.txt"
fastani_df = pd.read_csv(
    fastani_path, sep='\t',
    names=['query', 'ref', 'ani', 'mapped_kmers', 'total_kmers']
)
# Strip .fa suffix added by FastANI from genome filenames
for col in ('query', 'ref'):
    fastani_df[col] = fastani_df[col].str.replace(r'\.fa(sta)?$', '', regex=True)

samples = sorted(fastani_df['query'].unique())
n_samples = len(samples)

# Build symmetric ANI matrix (fill missing pairs with NaN)
ani_matrix = fastani_df.pivot_table(index='query', columns='ref',
                                    values='ani', aggfunc='first')
# Ensure symmetry: fill any missing (i,j) with (j,i)
for s in samples:
    for t in samples:
        if pd.isna(ani_matrix.loc[s, t]) and not pd.isna(ani_matrix.loc[t, s]):
            ani_matrix.loc[s, t] = ani_matrix.loc[t, s]
ani_matrix = ani_matrix.reindex(index=samples, columns=samples)

# --- NJ tree from FastANI ------------------------------------------
nwk_path = build_dir / "nj_tree" / "fastani_nj.nwk"
tt = toytree.tree(str(nwk_path))
tt = tt.ladderize()
leaf_order = tt.get_tip_labels()

# --- Alignment lengths per min_freq --------------------------------
align_rows = []
for mf_dir in sorted(explore_dir.glob("ska2/min_freq_*"),
                     key=lambda p: float(p.name.split('_')[2])):
    mf = float(mf_dir.name.split('_')[2])
    aln = mf_dir / "alignment.fasta"
    if not aln.exists():
        continue
    with open(aln) as fh:
        n_seqs = sum(1 for l in fh if l.startswith('>'))
    total_bases = sum(
        len(l.strip()) for l in open(aln) if not l.startswith('>')
    )
    align_rows.append({
        'min_freq': mf,
        'n_samples': n_seqs,
        'total_alignment_bases': total_bases,
        'per_sample_bases': total_bases // n_seqs if n_seqs else 0,
    })
_align_cols = ['min_freq', 'n_samples', 'total_alignment_bases', 'per_sample_bases']
align_df = pd.DataFrame(align_rows, columns=_align_cols) if align_rows else pd.DataFrame(columns=_align_cols)

# --- SNP sites counts per min_freq ----------------------------------
snp_rows = []
for mf_dir in sorted(explore_dir.glob("snpsites/min_freq_*"),
                     key=lambda p: float(p.name.split('_')[2])):
    mf = float(mf_dir.name.split('_')[2])
    sites_files = list(mf_dir.glob("*.sites.txt"))
    fas_files   = list(mf_dir.glob("*.fas"))
    if not sites_files or not fas_files:
        continue
    # sites.txt format: "A,C,G,T" constant counts
    acgt = list(map(int, sites_files[0].read_text().strip().split(',')))
    constant_sites = sum(acgt)
    # count SNP-site columns from the .fas file
    with open(fas_files[0]) as fh:
        seqs = [l.strip() for l in fh if not l.startswith('>')]
    snp_sites = len(seqs[0]) if seqs else 0
    snp_rows.append({
        'min_freq': mf,
        'constant_A': acgt[0], 'constant_C': acgt[1],
        'constant_G': acgt[2], 'constant_T': acgt[3],
        'constant_sites': constant_sites,
        'snp_sites': snp_sites,
        'total_sites': constant_sites + snp_sites,
        'snp_fraction': snp_sites / (constant_sites + snp_sites)
        if (constant_sites + snp_sites) > 0 else np.nan,
    })
_snp_cols = ['min_freq', 'constant_A', 'constant_C', 'constant_G', 'constant_T',
             'constant_sites', 'snp_sites', 'total_sites', 'snp_fraction']
snp_df = pd.DataFrame(snp_rows, columns=_snp_cols) if snp_rows else pd.DataFrame(columns=_snp_cols)

# Merge alignment and snp stats
stats_df = align_df.merge(snp_df, on='min_freq', how='outer').sort_values('min_freq')

# --- Gubbins per-branch statistics ---------------------------------
gubbins_data = {}
for mf_dir in sorted(explore_dir.glob("gubbins/min_freq_*"),
                     key=lambda p: float(p.name.split('_')[2])):
    mf = float(mf_dir.name.split('_')[2])
    csv_files = list(mf_dir.glob("*.per_branch_statistics.csv"))
    gff_files = list(mf_dir.glob("*.recombination_predictions.gff"))
    if not csv_files:
        continue
    stats = pd.read_csv(csv_files[0], sep='\t')
    # Count recombination events from GFF (skip header lines)
    n_recomb_blocks = 0
    if gff_files:
        with open(gff_files[0]) as fh:
            n_recomb_blocks = sum(
                1 for l in fh if l.strip() and not l.startswith('#')
            )
    gubbins_data[mf] = {'stats': stats, 'n_recomb_blocks': n_recomb_blocks}

available_gubbins_mf = sorted(gubbins_data.keys())
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Helper: toytree rendering
# ============================================================

def draw_toytree(tt, title=None, tip_font_size='10px', width=900):
    """Draw a toytree and return (canvas, html_str, svg_str)."""
    tips        = tt.get_tip_labels()
    tip_colors  = [tip_color(t) for t in tips]
    tip_sizes   = [9 if t in highlights else 5 for t in tips]

    # node_sizes / node_colors must span all nodes (tips + internal)
    n_internal  = tt.nnodes - tt.ntips
    all_colors  = tip_colors + ['#888888'] * n_internal
    all_sizes   = tip_sizes  + [0]         * n_internal
    height      = max(500, tt.ntips * 14)

    canvas, axes, mark = tt.draw(
        width=width, height=height,
        tip_labels=True,
        tip_labels_colors=tip_colors,
        tip_labels_style={'font-size': tip_font_size},
        node_sizes=all_sizes,
        node_colors=all_colors,
        node_style={'stroke': 'none'},
        layout='r',
    )
    if title:
        axes.label.text = title

    html_io = io.BytesIO()
    toyplot.html.render(canvas, html_io)
    html_str = html_io.getvalue().decode()

    svg_io = io.BytesIO()
    toyplot.svg.render(canvas, svg_io)
    svg_str = svg_io.getvalue().decode()

    return canvas, html_str, svg_str
```

## Run parameters {.unnumbered}

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import display, Markdown

display(Markdown(f"""
| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Run name | `{run_name}` |
| Build results | `{build_dir.resolve()}` |
| Explore results | `{explore_dir.resolve()}` |
| Samples | {n_samples} |
| Min-freq values tested | {sorted(stats_df['min_freq'].tolist())} |
| Gubbins results available for | {available_gubbins_mf} |
| Figures saved to | `{figs_dir.resolve()}` |
| Tables saved to | `{tables_dir.resolve()}` |
"""))
```

---

## 1. FastANI quality control

FastANI was run all-vs-all across all {n_samples} genomes. The NJ tree and pairwise ANI
heatmap below help identify potential outliers before proceeding to alignment.

::: {.callout-tip}
**What to look for:**

- Samples clustering away from the main group in the tree (long branches or separate clades)
  are candidate outliers — inspect their ANI values in the heatmap.
- Pairwise ANI < 95 % against most other samples suggests the genome may belong to a
  different species or subspecies.
- Groups of samples with notably lower inter-group ANI may indicate sub-populations worth
  investigating further.
:::

### 1.1 Neighbour-joining tree

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML

canvas, html_str, svg_str = draw_toytree(
    tt, title=f"FastANI NJ tree — {run_name}"
)
(figs_dir / "fastani_nj_tree.svg").write_text(svg_str)
(figs_dir / "fastani_nj_tree.html").write_text(html_str)

display(HTML(html_str))
```

### 1.2 Pairwise ANI heatmap (ordered by tree)

The rows and columns are ordered by the NJ tree leaf order so that clusters of similar
samples appear as blocks along the diagonal.

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Reorder matrix by tree leaf order (only keep leaves present in matrix)
ordered = [s for s in leaf_order if s in ani_matrix.index]
mat = ani_matrix.reindex(index=ordered, columns=ordered)

ani_min = np.nanmin(mat.values[mat.values < 100])  # exclude self-comparisons
ani_min = max(ani_min - 0.5, 90)                   # clamp floor

ticktext = [f"<b>{s}</b>" if s in highlights else s for s in mat.index.tolist()]

fig_heat = go.Figure(data=go.Heatmap(
    z=mat.values,
    x=mat.columns.tolist(),
    y=mat.index.tolist(),
    colorscale='Blues',
    zmin=ani_min,
    zmax=100,
    colorbar=dict(title='ANI (%)'),
    hovertemplate=(
        'Query: %{y}<br>'
        'Reference: %{x}<br>'
        'ANI: %{z:.4f}%'
        '<extra></extra>'
    ),
))
fig_heat.update_layout(
    **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    title=f"Pairwise FastANI — {run_name}",
    xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=8), tickangle=-45,
               ticktext=ticktext, tickvals=mat.columns.tolist()),
    yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=8), autorange='reversed',
               ticktext=ticktext, tickvals=mat.index.tolist()),
    height=max(600, n_samples * 13),
    width=max(650, n_samples * 13),
    margin=dict(l=160, b=160),
)
save_fig(fig_heat, "fastani_heatmap")
fig_heat.show()
```

### 1.3 ANI summary statistics

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Off-diagonal only
mask = np.ones(mat.shape, dtype=bool)
np.fill_diagonal(mask, False)
off_diag = mat.values[mask]
off_diag = off_diag[~np.isnan(off_diag)]

ani_stats = pd.DataFrame({
    'Statistic': ['Min ANI (%)', 'Mean ANI (%)', 'Median ANI (%)',
                  'Max ANI (%)', 'Std dev (%)'],
    'Value': [
        f"{off_diag.min():.4f}",
        f"{off_diag.mean():.4f}",
        f"{np.median(off_diag):.4f}",
        f"{off_diag.max():.4f}",
        f"{off_diag.std():.4f}",
    ]
})
# Per-sample mean ANI against all others
per_sample = mat.copy()
np.fill_diagonal(per_sample.values, np.nan)
sample_mean_ani = per_sample.mean(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=True)
sample_ani_df = sample_mean_ani.reset_index()
sample_ani_df.columns = ['Sample', 'Mean ANI (%) vs others']
sample_ani_df['Mean ANI (%) vs others'] = sample_ani_df['Mean ANI (%) vs others'].round(4)

ani_stats.to_csv(tables_dir / "fastani_summary_stats.csv", index=False)
sample_ani_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "fastani_per_sample_mean_ani.csv", index=False)

display(Markdown("**Summary statistics (all pairwise, self excluded):**"))
display(ani_stats.to_html(index=False, classes='table table-sm table-hover'))
display(Markdown(""))
display(Markdown("**Per-sample mean ANI against all others** (lowest first — potential outliers at top):"))
display(sample_ani_df.head(20).to_html(index=False, classes='table table-sm table-hover'))
```

---

## 2. Alignment statistics across `--min-freq` values

`ska align --min-freq X` includes only genome positions present in ≥ X × 100 % of samples.
Lower values retain more positions (including accessory/variable regions) with more
potential missing data per sample; higher values give a smaller but more complete alignment.

::: {.callout-tip}
**What to look for:**

- A steep drop in alignment length between two thresholds suggests many positions that are
  present in most but not all samples — these positions contribute most to the accessory genome.
- A plateau in SNP count at higher min_freq indicates diminishing informative sites in the
  strict core; if SNP count drops too low, IQ-TREE may have too little signal for a well-resolved tree.
- The fraction of variable sites (`SNP fraction`) should stay reasonable (≥ 0.1 % is typical).
- Choose a threshold where alignment length is sufficient for phylogenetics but missing data
  is not inflating the analysis. Inspect `gubbins/min_freq_*/` outputs for recombination
  predictions at candidate thresholds.
:::

```{python}
#| include: false
# Build summary table
table_df = stats_df[['min_freq', 'n_samples', 'per_sample_bases',
                      'snp_sites', 'constant_sites', 'snp_fraction']].copy()
table_df.columns = ['min_freq', 'N samples', 'Alignment length / sample (bp)',
                    'Variable sites', 'Constant sites', 'Variable fraction']
table_df['Variable fraction'] = table_df['Variable fraction'].map('{:.4f}'.format)
table_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "alignment_stats.csv", index=False)
```

### 2.1 Statistics table

```{python}
#| echo: false
display(table_df.to_html(index=False, classes='table table-sm table-hover'))
```

### 2.2 Alignment length and SNP count vs min-freq

```{python}
#| echo: false
mf_vals = stats_df['min_freq'].tolist()

fig_aln = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, shared_xaxes=True,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Alignment length per sample",
        "Variable sites (SNP columns)",
    ),
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
)
fig_aln.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=mf_vals,
        y=stats_df['per_sample_bases'],
        marker_color='steelblue',
        hovertemplate='min_freq=%{x}<br>Length=%{y:,} bp<extra></extra>',
    ),
    row=1, col=1,
)
fig_aln.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=mf_vals,
        y=stats_df['snp_sites'],
        marker_color='darkorange',
        hovertemplate='min_freq=%{x}<br>SNP sites=%{y:,}<extra></extra>',
    ),
    row=2, col=1,
)
fig_aln.update_layout(
    **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    title=f"Alignment statistics — {run_name}",
    showlegend=False,
    height=500,
)
fig_aln.update_xaxes(title_text='min_freq', row=2, col=1,
                     tickvals=mf_vals, ticktext=[str(v) for v in mf_vals])
fig_aln.update_yaxes(title_text='bp / sample', row=1, col=1)
fig_aln.update_yaxes(title_text='SNP columns', row=2, col=1)
save_fig(fig_aln, "alignment_length_and_snps")
fig_aln.show()
```

### 2.3 Site composition breakdown

Each bar shows the proportion of constant (A, C, G, T) vs variable sites at each threshold.

```{python}
#| echo: false
fig_comp = go.Figure()
bar_kwargs = dict(x=mf_vals, barmode='stack')
palette = {'A': '#4daf4a', 'C': '#377eb8', 'G': '#e41a1c', 'T': '#ff7f00', 'SNP': '#984ea3'}
for base in ('A', 'C', 'G', 'T'):
    fig_comp.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f'Constant {base}',
        x=mf_vals,
        y=stats_df[f'constant_{base}'],
        marker_color=palette[base],
        hovertemplate=f'min_freq=%{{x}}<br>Constant {base}=%{{y:,}}<extra></extra>',
    ))
fig_comp.add_trace(go.Bar(
    name='Variable (SNP)',
    x=mf_vals,
    y=stats_df['snp_sites'],
    marker_color=palette['SNP'],
    hovertemplate='min_freq=%{x}<br>SNP sites=%{y:,}<extra></extra>',
))
fig_comp.update_layout(
    **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    barmode='stack',
    title=f"Site composition — {run_name}",
    xaxis=dict(title='min_freq', tickvals=mf_vals,
               ticktext=[str(v) for v in mf_vals]),
    yaxis_title='Sites per sample',
    height=420,
)
save_fig(fig_comp, "site_composition")
fig_comp.show()
```

### 2.4 Variable-site fraction vs min-freq

```{python}
#| echo: false
fig_frac = go.Figure(go.Scatter(
    x=mf_vals,
    y=(stats_df['snp_fraction'] * 100).tolist(),
    mode='lines+markers',
    marker=dict(size=9, color='steelblue'),
    line=dict(width=2),
    hovertemplate='min_freq=%{x}<br>Variable fraction=%{y:.3f}%<extra></extra>',
))
fig_frac.update_layout(
    **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    title=f"Fraction of variable sites — {run_name}",
    xaxis=dict(title='min_freq', tickvals=mf_vals,
               ticktext=[str(v) for v in mf_vals]),
    yaxis_title='Variable sites / total sites (%)',
    height=350,
)
save_fig(fig_frac, "variable_site_fraction")
fig_frac.show()
```

---

## 3. Gubbins recombination detection

Gubbins was run on the subset of min_freq values that completed before this report was
generated. Results are available for: **`{available_gubbins_mf}`**.

::: {.callout-note}
Min-freq values with no Gubbins result directory were still running or did not complete.
:::

```{python}
#| include: false
# Build combined per-sample recombination table for all available min_freq values
tip_dfs = []
for mf, d in gubbins_data.items():
    df = d['stats'].copy()
    # Keep only tip nodes (internal nodes have names like Node_*)
    df = df[~df['Node'].str.match(r'^Node_\d+$', na=False)].copy()
    df['min_freq'] = mf
    df['n_recomb_blocks_total'] = d['n_recomb_blocks']
    tip_dfs.append(df)

recomb_df = pd.concat(tip_dfs, ignore_index=True) if tip_dfs else pd.DataFrame()
recomb_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "gubbins_per_sample.csv", index=False)
```

### 3.1 Recombination events detected

```{python}
#| echo: false
summary_rows = []
for mf, d in sorted(gubbins_data.items()):
    summary_rows.append({
        'min_freq': mf,
        'Recombination blocks in GFF': d['n_recomb_blocks'],
        'Samples in analysis': (~d['stats']['Node'].str.match(r'^Node_\d+$', na=False)).sum(),
    })
summary_gub = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)
summary_gub.to_csv(tables_dir / "gubbins_summary.csv", index=False)
display(summary_gub.to_html(index=False, classes='table table-sm table-hover'))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not recomb_df.empty and recomb_df['Number of Recombination Blocks'].sum() == 0:
    display(Markdown(
        "> **Gubbins detected no recombination events at any tested min_freq threshold.**\n"
        "> This can indicate a genuinely clonal population, or that the `--min-snps` "
        "> threshold is too high for the diversity present. "
        "> Try lowering `--min-snps` in Stage 3 if recombination is expected."
    ))
```

### 3.2 Total SNPs per sample (Gubbins tree annotation)

Even when no recombination is detected, the per-branch SNP counts from Gubbins' internal
tree are informative — samples with unusually high SNP counts may be divergent or
mis-assembled.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not recomb_df.empty:
    figs_snp = []
    for mf in sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique()):
        sub = recomb_df[recomb_df['min_freq'] == mf].sort_values(
            'Total SNPs', ascending=True
        )
        figs_snp.append(
            go.Bar(
                name=f'min_freq={mf}',
                x=sub['Total SNPs'],
                y=sub['Node'],
                orientation='h',
                hovertemplate='%{y}<br>Total SNPs: %{x:,}<extra></extra>',
                visible=(mf == sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]),
            )
        )
    fig_snps = go.Figure(data=figs_snp)

    mf_buttons = [
        dict(
            label=f'min_freq={mf}',
            method='update',
            args=[{'visible': [m == mf for m in sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())]},
                  {'title': f'Total SNPs per sample — min_freq={mf} — {run_name}'}],
        )
        for mf in sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())
    ]
    fig_snps.update_layout(
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
        title=f"Total SNPs per sample — min_freq={sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]} — {run_name}",
        xaxis_title='Total SNPs (Gubbins per-branch count)',
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9)),
        height=max(500, len(sub) * 14 + 100),
        updatemenus=[dict(
            buttons=mf_buttons,
            direction='down', showactive=True, x=0.01, y=1.12, xanchor='left',
        )],
        margin=dict(l=180),
    )
    save_fig(fig_snps, "gubbins_total_snps_per_sample")
    fig_snps.show()
```

### 3.3 r/m ratio per sample

The r/m ratio is the ratio of substitutions introduced by recombination to those introduced
by point mutation. Higher values indicate heavier recombination influence on a lineage.
Values are 0 when no recombination is detected.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not recomb_df.empty:
    rm_figs = []
    for mf in sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique()):
        sub = recomb_df[recomb_df['min_freq'] == mf].sort_values('r/m', ascending=True)
        rm_figs.append(go.Bar(
            name=f'min_freq={mf}',
            x=sub['r/m'],
            y=sub['Node'],
            orientation='h',
            hovertemplate='%{y}<br>r/m: %{x:.4f}<extra></extra>',
            visible=(mf == sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]),
        ))
    fig_rm = go.Figure(data=rm_figs)
    fig_rm.update_layout(
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
        title=f"r/m ratio — min_freq={sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]} — {run_name}",
        xaxis_title='r/m ratio',
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9)),
        height=max(500, len(sub) * 14 + 100),
        updatemenus=[dict(
            buttons=mf_buttons,
            direction='down', showactive=True, x=0.01, y=1.12, xanchor='left',
        )],
        margin=dict(l=180),
    )
    save_fig(fig_rm, "gubbins_rm_ratio")
    fig_rm.show()
```

### 3.4 Bases in clonal frame vs genome length

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not recomb_df.empty:
    cf_figs = []
    for mf in sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique()):
        sub = (
            recomb_df[recomb_df['min_freq'] == mf]
            .assign(clonal_pct=lambda d:
                    100 * d['Bases in Clonal Frame'] / d['Genome Length'].replace(0, np.nan))
            .sort_values('clonal_pct')
        )
        cf_figs.append(go.Bar(
            name=f'min_freq={mf}',
            x=sub['clonal_pct'],
            y=sub['Node'],
            orientation='h',
            hovertemplate=(
                '%{y}<br>'
                'Clonal frame: %{x:.1f}%<br>'
                '<extra></extra>'
            ),
            visible=(mf == sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]),
        ))
    fig_cf = go.Figure(data=cf_figs)
    fig_cf.update_layout(
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
        title=f"Clonal frame coverage — min_freq={sorted(recomb_df['min_freq'].unique())[0]} — {run_name}",
        xaxis=dict(title='Clonal frame (% of genome length)', range=[0, 105]),
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9)),
        height=max(500, len(sub) * 14 + 100),
        updatemenus=[dict(
            buttons=mf_buttons,
            direction='down', showactive=True, x=0.01, y=1.12, xanchor='left',
        )],
        margin=dict(l=180),
    )
    save_fig(fig_cf, "gubbins_clonal_frame")
    fig_cf.show()
```

---

## 4. Suggested additional diagnostics

The following diagnostics are not yet implemented in this report but are recommended
for a thorough stage-2 assessment:

**Per-sample completeness across min-freq**
:   For each sample and each min-freq threshold, compute the fraction of alignment
    positions that are non-gap/non-N. This reveals which samples drive missing-data
    inflation at a given threshold and may flag poorly assembled or contaminated
    genomes. Requires reading the full SKA2 alignment FASTA files (~100–200 MB each);
    the `pyfastx` package (available in this env) makes this fast.

**Pairwise SNP distance matrix**
:   Run with `--ska_distance true` to obtain `ska2/distances.tsv` and a NJ tree based
    on raw SNP counts rather than ANI. This is complementary to FastANI and more
    sensitive within closely related samples. Can be visualised with the same
    Plotly heatmap approach used above.

**ANI vs SNP distance scatter**
:   Plot pairwise ANI (from FastANI) against pairwise SNP distance (from `ska distance`)
    for each sample pair. Outlier pairs that have high ANI but unexpectedly high SNP
    distance may indicate assembly artefacts or recent recombination.

**Missing data per sample at chosen min-freq**
:   A bar chart of the number of gap/N positions per sample at the candidate min-freq
    threshold. Samples with >5–10 % missing data are candidates for removal via
    `--ska_delete_samples` before stage 3.

**Gubbins tree topology concordance**
:   Compare the topology of the Gubbins final tree (`*.final_tree.tre`) with the FastANI
    NJ tree using a normalised Robinson–Foulds distance (`Bio.Phylo` provides this).
    Large disagreements may indicate recombination obscuring true genealogy.

**Recombination block size distribution**
:   Parse `*.recombination_predictions.gff` to extract block lengths when recombination
    is present. A histogram of block sizes helps assess whether Gubbins is calling
    many short (potentially spurious) blocks or a few large (biologically plausible) ones.

**Genome-length outlier check**
:   Compare the `Genome Length` column from Gubbins per-branch statistics. Genomes
    significantly shorter or longer than the mean may be fragmented assemblies or
    contain contamination.

```{python}
#| include: false
# Save the main stats table for downstream use
stats_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "alignment_stats_full.csv", index=False)
print(f"Figures saved to: {figs_dir.resolve()}")
print(f"Tables saved to:  {tables_dir.resolve()}")
```
